title: Explaining Culture to Technology, Paul Ford | Compile 26
summary: Paul Ford discusses culture as a distributed, lossy prediction model and compares how magazines operate to how technology systems should be structured. He frames culture as an operating system for consciousness and commentary on risk in the tech industry.
writeup: Paul Ford frames culture as a distributed, lossy prediction model and treats it as an operating system for consciousness, drawing on the workings of magazine publishing to argue how technology systems should be structured. The talk connects prediction sets and system design, using the editorial mechanics of magazines as a lens on how technical systems organize information and absorb risk. It closes with commentary on how the tech industry handles risk.
